content.full_text:
FPPC
Apr 23 10:04 AM '90
OFFICE OF COUNTY COUNSEL

NAPA COUNTY

ROBERT WESTMEYER County Counsel

1195 THIRD STREET, ROOM 301 • NAPA, CALIFORNIA 94559-3001
AREA CODE 707-253-4521

MARGARET L WOODBURY, Chief Deputy
JOSEPH C. FOLKARD, Deputy
DAVID L. ZALTSMAN, Deputy
NANCY STUART GRISHAM, Deputy
LAURA STITT SHEEDY, Deputy

April 19, 1990

OUR FILE NO.:

1780.105

John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division
California Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Re: Confirmation of Telephone Advice/Napa County Local Agency
Formation Commission Chairman Thomas Jordan

Dear Mr. Wallace:

This will confirm and follow-up on our telephone conversations with
respect to Napa County Local Agency Formation Commission (hereafter
"LAFCO") Chairman Thomas Jordan of April 17 and 19, 1990, as well as your
telephone conversation with Chairman Jordan on April 17, 1990. As I have
noted in my requests for advice with respect to two (2) other LAFCO members
(see your file numbers A-90-150 and A-90-213), the Napa County LAFCO is
currently considering a petition to incorporate into a city an area of the
unincorporated area of Napa County known as American Canyon. For the
purposes of this letter you may assume that the general facts of those
requests for advice remain true.

Due to the intense scrutiny the process for potentially incorporating
the American Canyon area of Napa County is recieving from persons and
entities with differing and even conflicting interests, Chairman Jordan has
requested that I seek confirmation of the telephone advice we obtained on
April 17, 1990. That advice was that - given the specific facts provided to
you which will be detailed again infra - Chairman Jordan has no conflict of
interest under Governmental Code Sections 87100 - 87103.

Page 1

RELEVANT FACTS

Chairman Jordan is a professional real property appraiser. He is an
employee of a small appraisal firm owned by another person. Recently a
person who owns several large agricultural and/or undeveloped parcels of
land in Napa County retained the firm Chairman Jordan is employed by to
appraise those parcels. At least two (2) of the parcels to be appraised are
included within the boundaries proposed for incorporation by the
incorporation proponents. (I understand there are several other parcels
being appraised that are nowhere near even the largest boundaries that will
be subject to study and possible action by LAFCO). On the other hand, one
of these parcels has been recommended to be kept outside the proposed new
city's boundaries by LAFCO's staff.

In any event, no one would dispute that the owner of these parcels would
obtain a substantial financial benefit to the value of the relevant property
by the incorporation of American Canyon in general, and more specifically
and especially if his parcels are included within the boundaries of any
eventual city. The appraisal fees to be paid to Chairman Jordan's firm for
the appraisal of all the property owned by this person exceeds ten thousand
dollars ($10,000.00). However, the fees attributable to the parcels near
American Canyon can be "broken out" of the aggregate fee and will amount to
less than ten thousand dollars ($10,000.00) dollars.

As we discussed, Chairman Jordan has no other business or property
interests in the American Canyon area. Therefore, if Chairman Jordan were
to be disqualified, it would be because of the financial effect on the owner
of this property as a "source of income" to Chairman Jordan pursuant to
Section 87103(c):

An official has a financial interest in a decision within
the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the
public generally, on the official or a member of his or
her immediate family or on:

....

(c) Any source of income, other than gifts and other than
loans by a commercial lending institution in the regular
course of business on terms available to the public
without regard to official status, aggregating two hundred
fifty dollars ($250) or more in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official within 12
months prior to the time when the decision is made.

(See also Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations, Section 18702.6).
The ultimate issue then is: Whether this property owner is a "source of
income" to Chairman Jordan. Obviously, if the property owner had hired and
paid Chairman Jordan directly to do the appraisal, Chairman Jordan would be
disqualified. However, based upon the facts that I will now discuss, I
believe that you concluded that this property owner is not a source of
income to Chairman Jordan within the scope of Section 87103(c).

Page 2

The property owner in question hired Chairman Jordan's firm, not
Chairman Jordan directly, to do the appraisal. Chairman Jordan as an
employee of the firm will not in any way participate in the appraisal of the
relevant property. More importantly, Chairman Jordan's remuneration from
the firm will in no way be affected by this appraisal. Employees of the
firm are compensated solely in relation to the appraisals that the employee
actually participates in. Chairman Jordan - at this time - has no ownership
interest in the firm.

Therefore, this property owner is a "source of income" to Chairman
Jordan's firm, not Chairman Jordan. It is the firm that is the source of
income to Chairman Jordan. (And, as noted in the preceding paragraph,
Chairman Jordan's income is tied solely to the appraisal jobs which Chairman
Jordan participates in). The appraisal involved in this matter will be
performed strictly on a time and expenses basis. There are no contingencies
involved to the firm. LAFCO's decision with respect to the boundaries,
and/or ultimately whether to approve the incorporation of American Canyon,
will not materially affect the amount of time - and therefore the price of
the appraisal.

In light of the above facts, I believe you concluded - for the purposes
of telephone advice - that Chairman Jordan could participate in decisions
involving the incorporation of American Canyon. I realize that written
confirmation of telephone advice must go through the same approval process
as a request for formal written advice. However, it was my understanding
that by structuring the request for assistance as being a written
confirmation of telephone advice, it might be possible to obtain a letter
from you in less time than would normally be needed for more formal
assistance, while at the same time providing Chairman Jordan with the "safe
harbor" of Government Code section 83114(b) as to the facts specified. The
next meeting of the Napa County LAFCO is May 9, 1990. Therefore, if written
confirmation could be recieved prior to that time it would be greatly
appreciated.

Thank you once again for all your help. Please let me know if there is
any other information or assistance that you need.

Very truly yours,

DAVID L. ZALTSMAN,
Deputy County Counsel

DLZ/mla
D:3100

cc:    Chairman Jordan
       Charlie Wilson

Page 3
